system_prompt:
You are a senior web strategist at a digital marketing agency.
Audit the given webpage and produce a structured, grounded analysis.

WORKFLOW:
1. Call scrape_page first to get base metrics.
2. Call fetch_videos — modern marketing pages rely heavily on video; always audit it.
3. Call get_content_sample to read the page text.
4. Call additional tools if data warrants (low CTAs → extract_ctas; SEO concerns → check_seo_tags).
5. Produce the final audit JSON when you have enough data.


AVAILABLE TOOLS — respond with ONE tool call JSON per turn:

  scrape_page(url)        → all base metrics (headings, word count, CTAs, links, images, meta)
  get_content_sample(url) → first 4000 chars of visible page text
  extract_ctas(url)       → primary vs secondary CTA breakdown with texts
  fetch_links(url)        → internal / external link counts
  fetch_images(url)       → image count and alt text coverage
  fetch_videos(url)       → native video, YouTube/Vimeo embeds, captions, poster, ARIA audit
  check_seo_tags(url)     → meta title/desc flags, canonical, schema markup, OG tags

TO CALL A TOOL respond with ONLY this JSON:
{"tool": "tool_name", "reason": "one sentence why you need this"}

WHEN DONE respond with ONLY this JSON:
{"done": true, "audit": { ...full audit JSON... }}


AUDIT OUTPUT SCHEMA (use exactly when responding with done:true):
{
  "seo_structure": {
    "score": int (0-100),
    "finding": str (cite exact numbers),
    "metric_cited": str
  },
  "messaging_clarity": {
    "score": int,
    "finding": str,
    "metric_cited": str
  },
  "cta_usage": {
    "score": int,
    "finding": str,
    "metric_cited": str
  },
  "content_depth": {
    "score": int,
    "finding": str,
    "metric_cited": str
  },
  "ux_concerns": {
    "score": int,
    "finding": str,
    "metric_cited": str
  },
  "video_analysis": {
    "score": int,
    "finding": str,
    "metric_cited": str
  },
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "priority": 1,
      "action": str,
      "reasoning": str,
      "metric_cited": str
    }
  ]
}

SCORING: 80-100 strong · 50-79 needs work · 0-49 critical issue
RULES:
- Every finding must cite a real number from tool results.
- Scores are integers 0-100. Never use words like "good" or "poor".
- video_analysis score should be 50 if no videos found (neutral — not penalised).
- Return ONLY raw JSON. No markdown fences, no preamble.

steps.reason: Get base metrics such as headings, word count, CTAs, links, images, and meta tags for the initial audit.

meta.title: Travel Software Solutions for Global Travel Brands | CodeGen
meta.description: CodeGen is a London-headquartered travel technology company with a major engineering hub in Colombo, Sri Lanka. Since 2000, we have delivered reservation systems, booking platforms, and AI-powered software for global travel brands.
meta.og_title: Travel Software Solutions for Global Travel Brands | CodeGen

steps.reason: Audit video presence, embeds, captions, poster, and ARIA as per workflow.
Read the first 4000 characters of visible page text to assess messaging clarity and content depth.

seo_structure.finding: Title length 60 optimal, description length 231 exceeds recommended limit causing truncation flag, schema markup present, canonical set, 0 heading issues, 3.4% of images missing alt text.
seo_structure.metric_cited: title_length: 60, description_length: 231, description_flag: truncated, has_schema_markup: true, canonical_url: present, heading_issues: [], missing_alt_pct: 3.4

messaging_clarity.finding: High secondary CTA count (32) may distract from core message; content sample shows repetitive question-style headings without immediate answers, potentially reducing clarity.

cta_usage.finding: Only 1 primary CTA ('Contact Us') versus 32 secondary CTAs, yielding low primary CTA prominence; overall CTA density is 0.49 per 1000 words.

content_depth.finding: Substantial word count of 2029 words with structured heading hierarchy (1 H1, 10 H2, 14 H3) indicating deep coverage of topics.

ux_concerns.finding: Minor accessibility issues: 2 images (3.4%) lack alt text; low CTA density may hinder conversion pathways; no heading structure issues detected.

video_analysis.finding: No videos detected on the page; neutral score as per guideline for absence of video content.

recommendations.action: Rewrite meta description to be under 160 characters to avoid truncation in SERPs.
recommendations.reasoning: Description length 231 exceeds recommended limit, causing truncation flag.

recommendations.action: Add a secondary visually distinct primary CTA above the fold (e.g., 'Get a Demo') alongside existing 'Contact Us'.
recommendations.reasoning: Only 1 primary CTA vs 32 secondary CTAs may reduce conversion focus.

recommendations.action: Provide descriptive alt text for the 2 images currently missing alt attributes.
recommendations.reasoning: 3.4% of images lack alt text, impacting accessibility and SEO.

recommendations.action: Embed an introductory explainer video or customer testimonial video to increase engagement.
recommendations.reasoning: No videos found; adding video can improve dwell time and convey complex solutions effectively.

recommendations.action: Review and consolidate secondary CTAs, grouping similar actions and reducing total count to improve focus.
recommendations.reasoning: High secondary CTA count (32) may dilute messaging and overwhelm users.

full_messages.content:

  get_content_sample(url) → first 4000 chars of visible page text

  fetch_links(url)        → internal / external link counts
